exercises.title: Multi-Format Circuit Visualizer
exercises.description: Create a utility class that provides multiple visualization formats for debugging quantum circuits.
tasks: Implement text-based visualization with configurable fold width
Add matplotlib visualization with custom styling
Extract and display circuit statistics (depth, gate counts, qubit count)
Create a method to highlight specific gates by type
Generate a summary report combining visualization and statistics

qc.h(0)

exercises.solution:
from qiskit import QuantumCircuit
from typing import Dict, List, Optional

class DebugVisualizer:
    """Multi-format circuit visualizer for debugging."""
    
    def __init__(self, circuit: QuantumCircuit):
        self.circuit = circuit
    
    def text_view(self, fold: int = 80, show_idle: bool = True) -> str:
        """Generate ASCII text diagram."""
        return self.circuit.draw(
            output='text',
            fold=fold,
            idle_wires=show_idle,
            initial_state=True
        ).__str__()
    
    def get_statistics(self) -> Dict:
        """Extract circuit statistics."""
        gate_counts = dict(self.circuit.count_ops())
        return {
            'depth': self.circuit.depth(),
            'num_qubits': self.circuit.num_qubits,
            'num_clbits': self.circuit.num_clbits,
            'gate_counts': gate_counts,
            'total_gates': sum(gate_counts.values()),
            'num_parameters': self.circuit.num_parameters
        }
    
    def find_gates(self, gate_type: str) -> List[int]:
        """Find indices of all gates of specified type."""
        indices = []
        for i, (inst, qargs, cargs) in enumerate(self.circuit.data):
            if inst.name == gate_type:
                indices.append(i)
        return indices
    
    def summary_report(self) -> str:
        """Generate comprehensive debug summary."""
        stats = self.get_statistics()
        report = []
        report.append('=' * 60)
        report.append('CIRCUIT DEBUG REPORT')
        report.append('=' * 60)
        report.append('')
        report.append('STATISTICS:')
        report.append(f'  Depth: {stats["depth"]}')
        report.append(f'  Qubits: {stats["num_qubits"]}')
        report.append(f'  Classical bits: {stats["num_clbits"]}')
        report.append(f'  Total gates: {stats["total_gates"]}')
        report.append('')
        report.append('GATE BREAKDOWN:')
        for gate, count in sorted(stats['gate_counts'].items()):
            report.append(f'  {gate}: {count}')
        report.append('')
        report.append('CIRCUIT DIAGRAM:')
        report.append(self.text_view())
        return '\n'.join(report)
exercises.expectedOutput: A formatted report showing circuit statistics and ASCII diagram
hints: circuit.count_ops() returns gate counts as a dictionary
circuit.data contains all instructions with their arguments
Use string formatting for clean report output

exercises.title: Layer-by-Layer Circuit Analyzer
exercises.description: Build a tool that decomposes circuits into layers and allows inspection of each layer individually.
tasks: Implement layer extraction based on qubit dependencies
Create visualization for individual layers

# Test

qc.h([0, 1, 2, 3])  # Layer 0: parallel H gates
qc.cx(0, 1)          # Layer 1
qc.cx(2, 3)          # Layer 1: parallel with above

hints: Track which qubits are used in current layer
Start new layer when qubit conflict occurs

exercises.title: State Evolution Tracker

tasks: Compute statevector after each gate

exercises.title: Interactive Checkpoint Debugger
exercises.description: Build an interactive debugger that allows setting checkpoints and inspecting circuit state.
tasks: Implement checkpoint creation with named markers
Allow state inspection at any checkpoint
Compare states between two checkpoints
Generate diff reports showing state changes
Support conditional breakpoints based on probability thresholds
exercises.starterCode:
from qiskit import QuantumCircuit
from qiskit.quantum_info import Statevector
import numpy as np
from typing import Dict, List, Optional, Callable

class CheckpointDebugger:
    """Interactive checkpoint-based debugger."""
    
    def __init__(self, circuit: QuantumCircuit):
        self.circuit = circuit.remove_final_measurements(inplace=False)
        self.checkpoints: Dict[str, int] = {}
        self.breakpoint_conditions: Dict[str, Callable] = {}
    
    def add_checkpoint(self, name: str, after_gate: int):
        """Add a named checkpoint after specified gate index."""
        pass
    
    def add_conditional_breakpoint(self, name: str, after_gate: int,
                                   condition: Callable[[np.ndarray], bool]):
        """Add breakpoint that triggers when condition is True."""
        pass
    
    def get_state(self, checkpoint: str) -> Dict:
        """Get full state information at checkpoint."""
        pass
    
    def diff_checkpoints(self, cp1: str, cp2: str) -> Dict:
        """Generate diff between two checkpoints."""
        pass
    
    def check_breakpoints(self) -> List[str]:
        """Return list of triggered breakpoint names."""
        pass
    
    def run_to_checkpoint(self, name: str) -> Dict:
        """Execute circuit up to checkpoint and return state."""
        pass

# Test
qc = QuantumCircuit(2)
qc.h(0)
qc.cx(0, 1)
qc.x(0)

debugger = CheckpointDebugger(qc)
debugger.add_checkpoint('after_bell', 2)
debugger.add_checkpoint('final', 3)

print(debugger.diff_checkpoints('after_bell', 'final'))
exercises.expectedOutput: Diff showing probability changes between checkpoints
hints: Store checkpoints as name -> gate_index mapping
Build partial circuits for state computation
Compare probabilities between states for diff
